headline: Embedded Software Engineer | C/C++ firmware on ARM/FPGA/SoC/SoM, imaging systems, electro-optical/infrared sensors, board bring-up, flight testing
cityOrDetail: Boston, MA (open to onsite roles in Greater Boston, including Lexington)
otherLinks: https://alexrivera.dev/imaging-firmware-notes — board bring-up and embedded imaging case studies
targetRolesNarrative: I am seeking embedded software engineer roles on imaging and sensor teams where I design firmware for embedded hardware from concept to functional prototype, board bring-up, and system-level integration. I write C/C++ for ARM processors in modern FPGAs, SoCs, and SoMs; troubleshoot issues spanning electrical and software boundaries with oscilloscopes, DMM, logic analyzers, and debuggers; and collaborate with hardware, mechanical, firmware, and test engineers through ground and flight testing to mature prototypes into products. I am especially interested in computer vision, perception, electro-optical, infrared, and sensor fusion systems for defense technology and autonomy.
industryOtherNote: Actively seeking defense technology and gov-adjacent imaging roles. Defense sector is a target industry, not a dealbreaker.
companiesYouAdmire: Imaging and autonomy teams doing state-of-the-art embedded hardware/software work with disciplined cross-functional collaboration from prototype through ground and flight testing.
honestCareerNarrative: I studied electrical and computer engineering and began in robotics sensor modules, where I learned board bring-up, JTAG debugging, timers and interrupts, and low-level driver development on SPI, I2C, and CAN buses. More recently I have focused on imaging embedded systems: firmware for electro-optical and infrared sensor payloads on ARM/FPGA/SoC/SoM platforms, Xilinx HDL bring-up, embedded Linux device trees and bootloaders, and integration with computer vision and perception pipelines. I am comfortable using oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, current-probes, and software debuggers to solve problems that cross electrical and firmware boundaries, and I use Git/GitHub for configuration control across multi-product firmware development cycles.
proudestProfessionalWins:
Imaging prototype delivery: Led firmware for an embedded imaging prototype from concept through functional prototype, board bring-up, ground testing, and system integration with hardware, mechanical, and perception teams.

Cross-boundary debugging: Resolved complex issues spanning electrical design and C/C++ firmware using oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, current-probes, and structured software instrumentation.

Production readiness: Matured prototype firmware into a supportable product line with manufacturing diagnostics, automated test scripts, and robust regression tests across multiple product development stages.
gapsOrNonTraditionalPath: Undergraduate research in sensor fusion gave me early exposure to perception pipelines before my first industry embedded role.
resumePlainText:
Alex Rivera
Embedded Software Engineer — Imaging & Sensor Systems
Boston, MA | Open to relocation | Eligible for U.S. Secret clearance

SUMMARY
Embedded software engineer with 7+ years developing firmware for embedded processors using C/C++. Expertise in ARM processors in modern FPGAs, SoCs, and SoMs; electro-optical and infrared imaging systems; board bring-up; ground and flight testing; and system-level integration.

EXPERIENCE
Senior Embedded Software Engineer — AeroSense Imaging — Boston, MA | 2020 – Present
• Design firmware for embedded hardware from concept to functional prototype, board bring-up, and system-level integration.
• Write C/C++ for ARM processors in modern FPGA, SoC, and SoM platforms, including Xilinx FPGA HDL bring-up and embedded real-time systems with timers and interrupts.
• Troubleshoot issues spanning electrical and software boundaries using oscilloscopes, DMM, logic analyzers, current-probes, and software debuggers.
• Work closely with hardware, mechanical, software, firmware, and test engineers for component selection, troubleshooting, product support, and delivery of fully functional products.
• Develop firmware across multiple imaging products at various stages of the development cycle, including ground and flight testing.
• Support electro-optical and infrared sensor integration, computer vision/perception pipelines, and manufacturing diagnostics.
• Use Git/GitHub for configuration control and automated test automation for complex embedded systems.

Embedded Engineer — Northeast Robotics Lab — Cambridge, MA | 2017 – 2020
• Developed device drivers and middleware for sensor modules in C/C++ on embedded Linux, including device trees, hardware drivers, and bootloaders.
• Implemented SPI, I2C, CAN, and UART communication on embedded processors.
• Participated in prototype builds, EMI mitigation, and field validation for perception hardware.

PROJECTS
FPGA Imaging Bring-Up Platform — C/C++, ARM, Xilinx FPGA, HDL
Firmware and board bring-up framework for electro-optical/infrared imaging sensor prototypes with structured lab validation.

Manufacturing Diagnostics Firmware — C, ARM Cortex-M
Factory test firmware for sensor board verification, calibration workflows, and automated test scripts.

EDUCATION
BS Electrical and Computer Engineering — Northeastern University — 2017

TECH STACK
Embedded C/C++, ARM, Xilinx FPGA/SoC/SoM, HDL, board bring-up, electro-optical/infrared sensors, SPI/I2C/CAN/UART, timers/interrupts, embedded Linux device trees/bootloaders, oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, current-probes, Python/MATLAB test harnesses, Git/GitHub
skillsCoreNarrative: Deep experience in embedded C/C++ firmware for ARM processors in modern FPGAs, SoCs, and SoMs; Xilinx FPGA HDL; electro-optical and infrared imaging; computer vision and perception integration; SPI/I2C/CAN buses; timers and interrupts; embedded Linux device trees, bootloaders, and hardware drivers; board bring-up; ground and flight testing; oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, current-probes, and debuggers; Git/GitHub configuration control; and cross-functional work with hardware, mechanical, firmware, and test engineers to mature prototypes into products.
toolsOtherNote: C/C++, ARM, Xilinx FPGA/SoC/SoM, HDL, OpenOCD, JTAG, SPI/I2C/CAN, timers/interrupts, embedded Linux device trees/bootloaders, oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, current-probes, electro-optical/infrared sensors, Python/MATLAB HIL, Git/GitHub.
educationDetails: BS Electrical and Computer Engineering, Northeastern University (2017). Senior design on embedded sensor fusion and imaging prototypes.

projects.title: FPGA Imaging Bring-Up Platform
projects.summary: Firmware and board bring-up framework for electro-optical and infrared imaging sensor prototypes on ARM/Xilinx FPGA/SoC/SoM platforms. Includes HDL integration hooks, structured lab validation with oscilloscopes and logic analyzers, register maps, driver abstractions, and integration with computer vision/perception pipelines through ground testing.
projects.techStackExtra: C/C++, ARM, Xilinx FPGA, HDL, board bring-up, electro-optical/infrared sensors, SPI/I2C/CAN, lab tooling.
projects.impactMetrics: Reduced prototype bring-up time across three sensor board revisions.

projects.title: Manufacturing Diagnostics Firmware
projects.summary: Factory test firmware for embedded sensor board verification, calibration, and pass/fail reporting integrated with manufacturing workflows.
projects.techStackExtra: C, ARM Cortex-M, manufacturing diagnostics, Python host tools.
projects.impactMetrics: Improved first-pass yield on production sensor boards.

storyHardestTechnicalChallenge: An imaging prototype showed intermittent frame drops caused by DMA timing assumptions that only appeared under specific sensor modes. I used logic analyzers and firmware instrumentation to isolate the issue and reworked buffer lifecycle handling across ISR and task boundaries.
storyDisagreementOrConflict: Hardware wanted to defer calibration logic to post-manufacturing software, but field data showed drift issues. I proposed embedding minimal calibration hooks in firmware early, which reduced support burden later.
storyBiggestMistake: I once merged a driver change without running the full manufacturing test suite and missed a calibration regression. I made factory test parity a release gate afterward.
storyLeadingWithoutAuthority: Coordinated firmware, hardware, and test engineers to define a shared bring-up checklist used across imaging prototype milestones.
storyTightDeadline: Delivered a functional imaging prototype firmware baseline ahead of a demo milestone by prioritizing core sensor pipeline and deferring non-critical telemetry features.
storyConflictingPriorities: Balanced a critical board bring-up issue with ongoing manufacturing support by timeboxing factory issues and pairing with a hardware engineer on lab debugging.
storyProcessImprovement: Standardized register documentation and lab capture templates, reducing handoff friction between hardware and firmware teams.
storyDifficultFeedback: Was told my debug notes were too verbose for operators. I created concise runbooks with optional deep-dive appendices for engineers.
storyMentoringTeaching: Mentored a junior engineer through their first ARM/FPGA bring-up cycle and code review process for driver changes.
storyCrossFunctionalCollaboration: Worked with hardware, perception, and manufacturing teams to define interfaces and validation plans for a new imaging product line.
storyAmbiguousProblem: Field reports of sensor instability had unclear ownership across optics, firmware, and power design. I instrumented firmware traces and helped isolate a power-rail sequencing issue.
storyEthicalOrRiskTradeoff: Insisted on additional validation before shipping a diagnostic mode that could expose raw sensor streams outside controlled environments.
whatYouWantNextNote: Seeking embedded software engineer roles on imaging teams designing firmware from concept through prototype, board bring-up, ground and flight testing, and product maturation — especially electro-optical, infrared, computer vision, and defense technology sensor systems.
compensationExtraNote: Open to relocation for imaging, autonomy, or defense technology embedded roles requiring clearance eligibility.
workAuthOtherNote: US citizen authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship.
